parsed_review.summary: CP00-399 is a clean descriptor-only continuation of the RP13 payments-core slice (CP392–CP398 pattern), covering exactly the 10 planned units RP13.P03.M06.S05–S14 under micro-phase RP13.P03.M06 "Synthetic Fixture Set" with deliverable distribution security_audit:2, contract:2, implementation:2, test:3, hermes_evidence:1. Source was inspected directly via Read/Grep/Glob (summary-only prohibited due to security/audit deliverables). Registry binding, service descriptor builders, coverage/slice validators, the payments-core contract case set, the test suite, and the validator script are mutually consistent with the plan and manifest — no drift. The pack-level claim is production_ready:false with implementation_layer:descriptor and runtime_ready:false; the production_ready gate is NOT weakened and no payments/payment-matching/AR-aging/journal-entry or other closed runtime is opened. No-write/no-real-data/no-leak attestations are present and enforced (writes_product_state:false, omits_unauthorized_data:true, raw_payload_included:false, exposes_* all false). Authority boundary holds: Claude is a read-only independent reviewer and not final approver, no enterprise-trust claim is made from local validation, and Hermes is used only as a deterministic control-plane evidence descriptor (H13 packet), not product runtime (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt:false). CP178+ hardening preconditions are satisfied: readiness auth preflight passed with valid JSON, correct model (claude-opus-4-8)/effort(max)/read-only tools, and forbidden-evidence patterns are codified. The post-review artifacts (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are intentionally produced downstream of this review in the hardening sequence and their current absence is not a defect. No findings; pack may proceed to the remaining post-review closeout steps under human final approval.
